hypothesis: If a large language model is continually pre-trained on MOF-related articles and PubChem chemical data and then fine-tuned on QMOF and hMOF using MOFid text representations, then it will achieve lower mean absolute error on band-gap and gas-adsorption property predictions than baseline graph-based models, especially when the fine-tuning dataset is small, because the pre-training imbues domain-specific chemical knowledge and rich textual representations that improve predictive performance in low-resource settings.
